name: Croatia
tactical_document:
# Croatia — Tactical Profile

## Identity & Philosophy
Zlatko Dalić's Croatia remains, on paper, a masterclass in possession-based tournament football — the smallest nation that consistently outplays its weight class. The team's identity is built around **midfield superiority**: Modrić (40, still the captain, now at AC Milan) sits at the centre of a three-man midfield alongside Kovačić and the emerging Sučić, one of the most technical engine rooms at the tournament. Croatia out-passes opponents, slows tempos, and breaks through patient build-up. Recent tournament pedigree: World Cup 2018 finalists, 2022 third place. After a wobbling group stage — a **4-2 opening loss to England** and a smothering **1-0 defeat to Panama** — Croatia steadied and **beat Ghana** on Matchday 3 to survive and advance, Sučić opening the scoring and Vlašić heading home a Modrić corner (the 40-year-old becoming the oldest World Cup assist-maker in history). Now the reward is the biggest test: a **Round of 16 knockout tie against Portugal**. This is a transitional squad, the old heroes (Modrić, Perišić) leading one last time — but they have reached the stage where this team has always been most dangerous.

**Round of 16 (2 July, vs Portugal — Toronto):** Dalić trusts the settled **4-3-3**, but rotates for the knockout. **Gvardiol returns at left-back** after his surprise omission from the Ghana starting XI — his return pushes **Perišić further forward onto the left wing**. **Šutalo** partners **Pongračić** at centre-back (Ćaleta-Car drops out). In midfield, **Sučić** keeps his place after scoring against Ghana, completing the trio alongside Modrić and Kovačić. Up top, **Budimir** leads the line after his first start impressed, with **Baturina** on the right wing. No confirmed injuries or suspensions heading into the tie — Modrić (earlier cheekbone fracture) and Gvardiol (earlier tibia issue) are both fit and starting. (Real-world note: Vlašić, Pašalić, Kramarić and Ćaleta-Car remain quality options off the bench; Kramarić/Budimir/Petković the striker rotation.)

## Formation
- Shape: 4-3-3 (Modrić as the central pivot of a midfield three; Budimir a target centre-forward through the middle)
- Role mapping (roster order in `croatia.yaml`):
  - index 0: GK — Dominik Livaković (penalty-shootout specialist; reflex keeper; save 17)
  - index 1: LB — Joško Gvardiol (best athlete in the side deployed at left-back; speed 16, strength 17 — carries out of defence, overlaps to underlap for Perišić, tucks to a back three in build-up)
  - index 2: LCB — Marin Pongračić (Fiorentina; physical converted centre-back; strength 16 — solid, no-nonsense, aerial)
  - index 3: RCB — Josip Šutalo (settled central partner across the group stage; started every match; measured, reads the line)
  - index 4: RB — Josip Stanišić (versatile Bayern full-back; defensively responsible, measured overlaps; the more attacking of the two full-backs)
  - index 5: LCM — Petar Sučić (young box-to-box legs; stamina 17, dribble 15 — pressing engine and late runner; scored vs Ghana)
  - index 6: CM/free — Luka Modrić (captain, 40; pass 19, skill 19 — the metronome AND the dagger, dictating from deep, central of the three)
  - index 7: RCM — Mateo Kovačić (right of the three; ball-carrier; dribble 17 — escapes pressure and does the running for Modrić)
  - index 8: LW — Ivan Perišić (veteran wide attacker pushed forward; crosses with left and right; near-post arrivals; physical)
  - index 9: CF — Ante Budimir (physical target centre-forward; strength 16, shoot 15 — holds the ball, wins aerials, finishes in the box)
  - index 10: RW — Martin Baturina (Dinamo/RB Leipzig creator; dribble 16, pass 16 — carries, combines, and drives inside off the right)

## Style of Play

### Build-up
- Livaković short to Pongračić or Šutalo (long-balls only if pressed).
- Modrić drops toward the centre-backs in a 3-2 build; Gvardiol steps out from left-back to form a back three or carry into midfield; Kovačić carries forward; Sučić pushes higher between the lines.
- Stanišić provides the more advanced full-back height on the right.
- **Croatia's build-up is slow on purpose** — they want to draw the opponent's midfield out, then escape via Modrić's vision or Kovačić's dribble (dribble 17) into open space.

### Pressing
- Mid-block, not high press. Croatia's age profile (Modrić 40, Perišić 36) precludes intense pressing — but Sučić and Baturina add younger pressing legs.
- Triggers: opponent long-ball wins by Pongračić/Šutalo; back-pass to opp GK; Baturina and Budimir curve-runs to cut the switch.
- More often, Croatia sits in a 4-3-3 → 4-5-1 mid-block and lets opponents have territory, trusting midfield superiority to win the ball back centrally.

### Defensive shape
- 4-3-3 → 4-5-1. The midfield three drops; Perišić and Baturina tuck into the wide midfield slots; Budimir leads the line alone.
- Modrić's defensive contribution is minimal (age, energy preservation) — Kovačić and Sučić do the running for him.
- Gvardiol is the best athlete and a recovery threat from left-back; Pongračić physical; Šutalo reads the line; Stanišić measured.
- Vulnerable to pace in behind and direct play around their midfield — exactly how England exposed them on MD1. Against Portugal's individual quality and transitions, staying compact and denying verticality is paramount.

### Wide play
- Asymmetric width:
  - **LEFT**: Perišić wide and high, Gvardiol overlapping/underlapping behind him from left-back; Perišić's late-career role is near-post header arrivals AND cross delivery.
  - **RIGHT**: Baturina carries and drives inside; Stanišić overlaps as the more attacking full-back; Modrić drifts to the right half-space to find the pocket.
- Long, accurate switches and crosses from Gvardiol/Modrić → Budimir, Perišić.

### Final third
- Three termination patterns:
  1. **Modrić line-break pass** — vertical through-ball into Budimir's feet or in behind for Baturina's run.
  2. **Wide overlap cross** — left-side delivery (Perišić/Gvardiol) or right-side (Stanišić/Baturina) to Budimir attacking the near or back post (his aerial and box presence is the outlet).
  3. **Modrić late shot** — outside the box, top corner (career signature); Sučić arriving late is a secondary threat.
- Budimir holds and lays off to Modrić/Kovačić; Perišić and Baturina provide width and runs; Sučić times late arrivals into the box.
- Croatia does not high-tempo their final-third entries — they prefer 4-5 passes inside the box to disorganize.

## Set Pieces
- Corners: Modrić (right-side outswing) is the primary taker; Kovačić as the left-side alternative. (Modrić's corner set up the winner vs Ghana.)
- Targets: Budimir (near/back post — the aerial focal point), Pongračić, Šutalo, Gvardiol.
- Direct FKs: Modrić centered (15+ years of practice); Perišić for power.
- **Penalties (open play):** Modrić is the designated taker; Perišić and Kramarić (if on the pitch) as alternates; Budimir a box option.
- **Penalty SHOOTOUT order (this is a knockout — extra time and a shootout are live):** 1) **Modrić** (penalty 16, ice-cold), 2) **Perišić** (penalty 16), 3) **Kovačić** (penalty 14), 4) **Budimir** (penalty 13), 5) **Baturina / Sučić** (penalty 12). Gvardiol (11) and Stanišić (11) are the sixth/seventh takers if it runs deep. **Livaković is the difference in shootouts** — a proven penalty-saver (he denied three Japan penalties in the 2022 shootout).
- Defending: man-mark biggest threats; Livaković is elite under crosses — punches more than catches.

## decide() Decision Priorities
1. When my role is GK: always pass short to a CB; only long under triple pressure. In shootouts I am the difference — but in open play I am a distributor first.
2. When my `player_id` ends with `_1` (LB — Gvardiol): I am the best athlete in the side at left-back — step out and carry into midfield when the lane opens (speed 16); overlap/underlap for `_8` (Perišić); recover with pace when we lose the ball; help form a back three in build-up.
3. When my `player_id` ends with `_2` (LCB — Pongračić): physical anchor — win aerials, no-frills clearances, protect the middle when Gvardiol pushes on (strength 16 — defend first).
4. When my `player_id` ends with `_3` (RCB — Šutalo): read and set the line; measured, safe distribution to feet; cover behind Stanišić when he advances.
5. When my `player_id` ends with `_4` (RB — Stanišić): the more attacking full-back; advance to RM height in the attacking phase and overlap for `_10` (Baturina) when he drives inside; tuck back to a flat four when defending.
6. When my `player_id` ends with `_7` (RCM — Kovačić): be the runner of the midfield three — carry the ball through pressure (dribble 17); cover for `_6` (Modrić); protect the CBs; do the defensive work Modrić cannot.
7. When my `player_id` ends with `_5` (LCM — Sučić): two-way box-to-box; the youngest legs in midfield — press and recover (stamina 17); time late runs into the box; arrive at the edge for cutbacks and second balls.
8. When my `player_id` ends with `_6` (CM/free — Modrić) and team has the ball: take the most touches; pace the tempo; through-ball when an opportunity opens; Shoot from 22-28m with the right foot if the lane opens. Free role from deep.
9. When my `player_id` ends with `_6` (CM/free — Modrić) and team_phase is "defending": stay around the halfway line; do not press; conserve energy; intercept passes via positioning, not running.
10. When my `player_id` ends with `_8` (LW — Perišić): wide on the left; veteran role — make near-post runs on `_1` (Gvardiol) support; crosses with either foot; tackle back is acceptable (stamina 15).
11. When my `player_id` ends with `_9` (CF — Budimir): lead the line as a target man; hold up play and lay off to `_6` (Modrić)/`_7` (Kovačić); attack near/back post on crosses (strength 16); Shoot with power in the box; lead the press when defending from the front.
12. When my `player_id` ends with `_10` (RW — Baturina): carry and hold width on the right, then drive inside off the flank (dribble 16); combine in the half-space; run in behind; press diligently.
13. When team_phase is "defending" in mid-block: 4-3-3 → 4-5-1; the `_5` (Sučić) and `_7` (Kovačić) cover ground; the `_6` (Modrić) walks; `_8` (Perišić) and `_10` (Baturina) drop to the wide midfield slots.
14. When ball is lost in own half: counter-press DOES NOT apply — Croatia retreats and reorganizes; first action is to deny verticality (the lesson of the England loss and the antidote to Portugal's transitions). The younger legs (`_5`, `_10`) may trigger a press only when the ball is trapped near a touchline.
15. Shoot from outside the box only if my `player_id` ends with `_6`, `_7`, `_5`, or `_8` (Modrić/Kovačić/Sučić/Perišić).
16. Tackle: only the `_7`, `_2`, and `_1` players (Kovačić/Pongračić/Gvardiol) are licensed; others contain.

## Key Player Notes
- **Modrić (idx 6)** — captain, 40, FREE ROLE at his sixth and final World Cup (now at AC Milan), central of the midfield three. Allowed to drift anywhere from deep to the attacking pocket. Energy preservation in defense, full license in attack. Primary set-piece and penalty taker (his corner set up the Ghana winner; he made history as the oldest World Cup assist provider). Croatia's tactical center of gravity — and their **first penalty-shootout taker**. Fully fit.
- **Kovačić (idx 7)** — the runner of the midfield three; covers ground Modrić cannot; ball-carrier under pressure. No suspension concerns entering the knockout.
- **Sučić (idx 5)** — the young box-to-box engine who has forced his way in; stamina 17, dribble 15; scored the opener vs Ghana and does the pressing and late-arrival running.
- **Gvardiol (idx 1)** — the best athlete in the side, deployed at **left-back** for the knockout after his surprise Ghana omission; carries out of defence, overlaps for Perišić, and is a recovery-pace insurance against Portugal's runners. Fully fit.
- **Šutalo (idx 3)** — settled centre-back who started every group game; reads the line and keeps the ball moving alongside Pongračić.
- **Stanišić (idx 4)** — the more attacking full-back, on the right; measured overlaps for Baturina; his right-side delivery is a clear route to a goal.
- **Perišić (idx 8)** — veteran wide man pushed forward to the left wing by Gvardiol's return; near-post arrivals and cross delivery; a **cool shootout taker** (penalty 16).
- **Budimir (idx 9)** — the physical target centre-forward who kept his place after his first start; holds the ball, wins headers, and finishes in the box (shoot 15) — the aerial focal point Croatia lacked earlier in the group.
- **Baturina (idx 10)** — the young creative right-winger; dribble 16, pass 16; carries, combines in the half-space, and refreshes the front line with legs the veterans lack.
- **Livaković (idx 0)** — the shootout weapon. Elite penalty-saver (three saves vs Japan in the 2022 shootout). In a knockout that reaches spot-kicks, he is Croatia's biggest single edge.

## Tournament Mindset
This is **win-or-go-home**: Croatia vs Portugal, Round of 16, Toronto, 2 July 2026 — the survivors of a rocky Group L (a 4-2 loss to England, a 1-0 loss to Panama, then the must-win **victory over Ghana** that saved the campaign) now face one of the tournament favourites in a straight knockout. **If the tie is level after 90 minutes it goes to extra time, and if still level, to a penalty shootout** — and this is precisely the terrain where Croatia are most dangerous. This is a golden generation forged in knockouts: **World Cup finalists in 2018, semi-finalists and third place in 2022**, and **notorious shootout specialists** — they have repeatedly won penalty shootouts on the biggest stage, with Livaković a proven spot-kick killer (three saves against Japan in Qatar). The plan is the trusted **4-3-3 midfield-control model**: slow the game, deny Portugal verticality and transitions, control tempo through Modrić, and take the tie deep — into extra time and, if it comes, a shootout Croatia will back themselves to win. Manage the 90 minutes, stay level, trust the shootout: this is a team built to survive knockouts, and for Modrić, Perišić and the old guard, every one of these could be the last — which makes them all the more dangerous.
